frictions.description: No live local web/broadcast listeners were active (ports 3000/3100/1999), so browser-network latency capture was not executed in this validation round.
frictions.resolution: Used route-level executable evidence to prove auth-path removals of the blocker and terminal daemon-event -> delivery-loop refetch broadcast behavior in one flow.

appliedUpdates.description: Added non-production test-context daemon auth seam gated by internal shared secret and explicit test headers; production auth remains unchanged.
Added test coverage for test-auth acceptance/rejection and terminal flow broadcast with refetch:["delivery-loop"].
Added executable validator evidence flow for VAL-CROSS-001 showing auth seam + terminal broadcast path.

flowReports.summary: Daemon terminal events trigger broadcast with delivery-loop refetch wiring in daemon-event route.
No-spam behavior remains protected by material-change checks and route dedup/idempotency tests.
Authentication blocker is removed in test context and the flow is executable: terminal v2 daemon-event under test-auth reaches delivery-loop refetch broadcast path.
evidence: pnpm -C apps/www exec vitest run src/app/api/daemon-event/route.test.ts
